title: Linked resources and loading hints

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Linked resources and loading hints; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Linked resources and loading hints
sources.claim: Stylesheets, scripts, icons, and preload hints declare external resources with loading behavior that can affect rendering, execution order, security, and performance.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Stylesheets, scripts, icons, and preload hints declare external resources with loading behavior that can affect rendering, execution order, security, and performance.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A noncritical module script loads with module semantics while the main stylesheet remains a normal render dependency. Your goal is to configure document resources and explain their loading relationship. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Document foundations; Text and page semantics; Forms and native controls; Relative and absolute URLs; Standards-mode document skeleton. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Stylesheets, scripts, icons, and preload hints declare external resources with loading behavior that can affect rendering, execution order, security, and performance. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Preload can waste bandwidth, blocking scripts delay parsing, integrity and CORS interact, and module scripts follow different dependency behavior. This draft therefore rejects: Adding preload to every resource always improves page speed.

includes: Link icons, styles and selected resource hints without redundant or harmful preloading.
Configure document resources and explain their loading relationship.
excludes: Preload can waste bandwidth, blocking scripts delay parsing, integrity and CORS interact, and module scripts follow different dependency behavior.

outcomes: Configure document resources and explain their loading relationship.

examples.title: Linked resources and loading hints: concrete case
examples.situation: A noncritical module script loads with module semantics while the main stylesheet remains a normal render dependency.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A noncritical module script loads with module semantics while the main stylesheet remains a normal render dependency.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Declare only measured critical resource hints and understand each resource's fetch and execution behavior.
examples.result: The result follows because Declare only measured critical resource hints and understand each resource's fetch and execution behavior.

walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Preload can waste bandwidth, blocking scripts delay parsing, integrity and CORS interact, and module scripts follow different dependency behavior.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Adding preload to every resource always improves page speed.
